title: adhatoda adenostachya
intro: Adhatoda adenostachya, also known as Adhatoda vasica, is a plant species in the family Acanthaceae, native to the Western Ghats of India. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant activities. The health benefits of Adhatoda adenostachya include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Adhatoda adenostachya is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, decoctions, and extracts, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments.
